Topic description: Debating a CS/ML master’s versus working, weighing pay (job hopping vs degrees), WLB, management prospects, opportunity cost, big-tech pathways, and strategies to maximize compensation.

Topic description: Advice for international MSCS students seeking data/ML roles: optimize resume with specific skills, write strong cover letters, leverage LinkedIn/GitHub networking, and apply widely to increase callbacks and offers.

Topic description: Choosing between Computer Science and Mechanical Engineering to build robots, machines, vehicles, and AI-driven tech.

Topic summary: Resume/Portfolio Strategy for Software & Data Science Projects
Topic description: Guidance on selecting, building, and presenting Python/AWS, EDA, computer vision, and embedded projects for resumes/portfolios, metrics to include, and interview discussion points.

Topic summary: Study Abroad Career Advice
Topic description: IR/CS students seeking master’s abroad and jobs (UN/NGO/tech), with limited experience, language skills, visa/work-permit concerns, and mental health considerations.

Topic description: Debate on calculus necessity in CS: foundational understanding helpful; mandatory for physics, graphics, ML, advanced stats; less critical for typical software/business analytics courses and roles

Topic summary: Transitioning from Engineering/Bioinformatics to SWE/ML
Topic description: Posts seeking advice on moving from mechanical/bioinformatics/electrical engineering to software or ML roles, covering skills, credentials, interviews, startups vs big tech, geography, and job satisfaction.

Topic description: Debate on integrating coding in schools, citing teacher shortages, pay gaps, oversaturation myths, cross-disciplinary benefits, and rising workforce demand in an AI-driven economy.

Topic description: Guidance for aspiring CS students: complement theory with personal projects, explore domains, pursue internships, weigh specialization vs generalist skillsets, and choose paths aligning with interests, styles, and career options

Topic description: Discussions of feeling like a genuine impostor in software/data roles, gaps in CS fundamentals, strategies to upskill, career transitions, and success stories overcoming tutorial hell to land jobs.
